# National ROW

> Eminent domain and condemnation consulting for property owners nationwide. National ROW represents property owners — never the condemning agency — to win the full just compensation they are owed in highway, utility, transmission, pipeline, transit/rail, solar, wind, data center, water, and every other kind of right-of-way or easement taking. On the property owner's side since the 1980s. National ROW is a consulting firm, not a law firm.

National ROW helps property owners whose land is being taken — in full or in part — for a public right-of-way or easement by a government agency, utility, transmission company, pipeline company, or transit authority. We carry the condemnation process for the owner: we assemble the expert team (condemnation appraisers, land planners, relocation specialists) and coordinate independent licensed attorneys when a case needs them, document what the property and the damages are truly worth, and deal with the agency directly. We are a consulting firm, not a law firm, and we do not provide legal advice.

## Key facts
- Service area: United States (nationwide, all 50 states). Headquartered in Texas.
- In business since the 1980s — more than 35 years representing property owners exclusively.
- We represent property owners only — never the condemning agency, utility, pipeline, or transit authority.
- Right-of-way and easement types: highway and road takings, electric transmission lines (including 765kV extra-high-voltage), natural gas and oil pipelines, water and drainage easements, utility easements, access easements, telecom/fiber right-of-way, solar farm easements, wind farm easements, transit and rail condemnation (light rail, commuter rail, BRT), data center power infrastructure ROW, and full fee takings.
- Phone: (469) 484-7960 and (956) 363-4144. Email: info@nationalrow.com
- Zero upfront cost for most property owners — contingency or flat-fee basis only.
- Spanish-language service available: https://nationalrow.com/es/

## How we respond to a condemnation (step by step)
1. Free case review — we assess the notice, the offer, and the property at no cost.
2. Independent valuation — we assemble condemnation appraisers to establish true market value, damages to the remainder, and all compensable impacts.
3. Documentation — every element of the claim: land taken, easement restrictions, access changes, building impacts, crops, and business losses.
4. Negotiation — we deal directly with the agency, utility, or pipeline on the owner's behalf using the independent appraisal as leverage.
5. Resolution — most cases resolve through negotiation. When they don't, we coordinate litigation support with independent licensed attorneys.

## Core guides
- Do I have to accept the first offer?: No. The agency's initial offer is a starting point, not a final number. Owners generally have the right to contest the valuation and pursue higher compensation for what is taken and for damage to what they keep.
- Partial takings and damages to the remainder: When only a strip is taken, the owner may also be owed compensation for the lost value of the land they keep — often the largest and most overlooked part of a claim.
- Easement vs. fee taking: An easement lets a utility or pipeline use a strip while the owner keeps title (with restrictions); a fee taking transfers ownership. Both are compensable, and the easement terms matter.
- Just compensation: Generally the fair market value of what is taken plus the reduction in value to what remains.
- Is National ROW a law firm?: No. National ROW is a right-of-way and condemnation consulting firm, not a law firm, and does not provide legal advice; it coordinates independent licensed attorneys when needed.


## Active major projects (2024-2026)
- ERCOT 765kV Statewide Transmission Expansion (Texas): ERCOT's Long-Range Transmission Plan includes new 765kV extra-high-voltage lines crossing West Texas, Central Texas, South Texas, and the Gulf Coast. AEP Texas and Oncor are acquiring ROW across hundreds of miles of private land. National ROW represents landowners along every 765kV corridor statewide. See: https://nationalrow.com/states/texas/#765kv
- Purple Line Light Rail (Maryland & D.C.): 16-mile light-rail condemnation cases, active. See: https://nationalrow.com/services/transit-rail-condemnation/
- Matterhorn Express Pipeline (TX): West Texas natural gas line.
- President George W. Bush Turnpike (Dallas-Fort Worth, TX): Ongoing expansion.
- Duke Energy Grid Modernization (NC): Transmission ROW upgrades.
- Galveston Seawall (TX): Gulf Coast flood-protection condemnations.
- IH-35 Capital Express (Austin, TX): Multi-phase highway expansion.
- Ports-to-Plains / I-27 and Forts-to-Ports / I-14 (TX): Major Texas corridor projects.
- I-44 Interchange Expansion (Tulsa, OK): Oklahoma highway condemnations.
- SH 99 Grand Parkway (Houston, TX): Ongoing toll road ROW.

## FAQ

- What does National ROW cost?: No upfront cost for most property owners. Contingency or flat fee — if we don't win more than the agency's offer, there is no fee.
- Do I have to accept the condemnation offer?: No. The agency's first offer is an opening position. You have the right to get an independent appraisal and negotiate. Most clients receive significantly more than the initial offer.
- What is just compensation?: Fair market value of the property or easement taken plus all damages to the land you keep — loss of access, severance, business losses, crop losses, and any other compensable impacts under state law.
- What is a partial taking?: When only part of your property is condemned, you keep the rest but may be owed damages to the remainder if the taking reduces its value.
- Does National ROW handle 765kV transmission line easements?: Yes. We represent landowners facing AEP Texas, Oncor, Duke Energy, NextEra, and other utilities on high-voltage easements, including every ERCOT 765kV corridor across Texas.
- Can a private pipeline company condemn my land?: Yes, in most states pipeline companies hold eminent domain authority. You are still entitled to just compensation and the right to negotiate.
- What if I've already signed an offer?: Contact us anyway. Depending on the agreement and state law, there may still be options. Free review.
- My property isn't in Texas — can you help?: Yes. We work nationwide in all 50 states.
